blocks.content: She reached up and touched his face.
Not the way you touch something to check if it's real. The way you touch something because you know it is and you want to hold the knowing.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I want you to know that I know it does.
She laughed very quietly — the soft kind, the kind that releases tension without breaking it — and he kissed her.
The fire behind them. The dark mountain outside. The card on the counter, their names written together in her father's hand.
The bedroom door was ajar, the bed with linen turned down, the lamp throwing one warm circle of light.
